old_claim.note: Pigment Creation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The Blombos drawing was made with red ochre pigment intentionally applied by an ochre crayon, making pigment creation and use the documented material enabler for this scoped graphic-symbol node.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: reports a cross-hatched pattern drawn with an ochre crayon on a ground silcrete flake from approximately 73,000-year-old Blombos Cave levels and says red ochre pigment was intentionally applied.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source ties the scoped early graphic sign to intentional application of red ochre pigment.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Pigment creation is enabling rather than universal because some symbolic signs are engraved, but the dated anchor for this node uses ochre pigment directly.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: pigment_creation remains an enabling prerequisite, now with a primary-source rationale.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to non-pigment engravings only.
The pigment_creation node were narrowed away from ochre pigment production and use.
A stronger source showed the Blombos cross-hatched pattern was not intentionally applied pigment.
short_reason: The Blombos drawing uses intentionally applied red ochre pigment.
